metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: They lived fast, and they died young. And they did it at a time when the universe should have been nothing a nursery.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: The implication is that star formation in the early universe was not gradual. It was catastrophic.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: For nearly a decade, astronomers have measured the expansion rate of the universe using

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: two incompatible answers. This discrepancy is called the Hubble tension, and the James Webb Space Telescope was

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: supposed to resolve it. It did the opposite. The cosmic microwave background, the afterglow of the Big Bang

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: mapped by the Planck satellite, predicts the universe should be expanding at 67 .4 km per

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: second per megaparsec. A megaparsec is about 3 .26 million light years,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: so this number tells you how much faster distant objects are flying away from us the farther out you look.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: second per megaparsec. The gap between these numbers is roughly 9%. The hope was that Hubble's were

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: slightly wrong. Distant stars crowded together, dust dimming the light, subtle errors compounding over billions of

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: light years. The James Webb Space Telescope has 4 times the resolution of Hubble, and 10 times the sensitivity.

scenes.text: data almost exactly. A value of 72 .6 km per

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: second per megaparsec from Webb compared to 72 .8 from Hubble. The difference is under 2%.

scenes.text: measurement error is gone. The tension remains. The universe is expanding at
